name: ssh-endpoint-port-mtu
doc: Pins the SSH-deploy + endpoint_port-override + per-node-MTU byte surfaces that no other corpus fixture exercises. router-a carries an ssh_alias (deploy resolves to the bare alias, SSHPort 0 -> ssh_config decides) and a non-zero MTU (1380) -> the per-peer/router install.sh MTU echo branch (script.go:777). router-b carries ssh_host+ssh_user+ssh_port+ssh_key_path where the key path has a SPACE ('~/keys/My Deploy Key.pem', charset-legal) -> exercises the bashSingleQuote/powerShellArgQuote '-i <path>' quoting idiom in deploy-all.sh/.ps1 (deploy.go buildSSHOpts) plus the user@host + custom-port target. The router-a<->router-b edge sets an explicit endpoint_port (52000) -> CompiledPort override flows into router-a's wireguard peer Endpoint = router-b.example.com:52000 (compiler.go:233). client-a is a client-role node with MTU 1280 -> the client install.sh MTU echo branch (script.go:1425). The SSH fields are project-level (deploy scripts), the MTU + endpoint_port are per-node/per-file (install.sh + wireguard conf) — all in the pinned conformance byte set.
